user_prompt:
<command-message>loop</command-message>
<command-name>/loop</command-name>
<command-args>Drain the bd ready queue using the Blackrim standard pace of work (CLAUDE.md "Standard pace"). Each iteration:

1. Run `bd ready --json` to survey work; if empty, exit the loop.
2. Group by file-conflict scope; size the next wave dynamically (3–8 agents, default 5).
3. Claim atomically (`bd update <id> --claim`) before any spawn.
4. Spawn workers in parallel — one Agent-tool call per scope group, all in a single response, each with `isolation: "worktree"`, `run_in_background: true`. Brief each tightly: bd ID(s), file scope, acceptance criteria, constraints, worktree-isolation reminder, wiring + docs standard.
5. Route by advisor — let the dispatch rules pick tier; default sonnet for builds, haiku for research/writes, opus only when `opus_required=true` is justified. Goal is minimum LLM token cost; do not run mechanical work directly on Gestalt-opus.
6. As agents finish (ta[REDACTED]), merge with `bin/blackrim-merge-agent` and spawn replacements to sustain ~5 in flight.
7. Self-pace via ScheduleWakeup: while agents are running, sleep ~270s (under cache TTL). Between waves with idle time, sleep 1200–1800s. Wake earlier on agent-completion notifications.

Stop conditions: `bd ready` returns empty, OR user sends a message in chat, OR a real merge conflict / unknown verdict requires opus judgment. On stop, report queue status + insights worth capturing.</command-args>
